title: MVP Core Tracker Proposal
executive_summary: Ship a production-ready crypto savings tracker focused on personal goal management, asset assignment, optional on-chain wallet tracking, fiat/manual transactions, and clear dashboards, while this revision closes the iteration-8 review blockers with four concrete changes: a cross-cutting retirement matrix now names every retained producer and retired consumer for planning notifications, sharing refresh, reminder scheduling, and widget-host cleanup; the Apple shell now has one explicit root-state contract with onboarding handoff and macOS split-view selection rules; the proposal itself now contains the authoritative visual language and named error-state treatments so external comps are translation/verification artifacts rather than a separate design dependency; and visionOS scope is corrected to compile-only parity because repo guidance says platform capabilities exist but end-user UI is not yet implemented.

target_user.definition: An individual user who wants to track one to five savings goals, connect a small number of crypto wallets or fiat accounts, and understand real progress without managing household collaboration, budgeting systems, or forecasting workflows.
jobs_to_be_done: Create a goal in minutes and understand the target, deadline, and current progress.
Attach crypto or fiat assets to a goal and optionally track an on-chain address.
Record manual transactions when funds move outside automatic wallet tracking.
Open the dashboard and immediately see what needs attention next.
explicitly_not_for: Household or family collaboration on shared goals.
Users who primarily want budget planning, forecast modeling, or notification-driven automation.
Power users who expect CSV workflows, local bridge sync, or deep scenario analysis in the MVP release.

value_proposition: CryptoSavingsTracker MVP helps a single user create named savings goals, connect crypto or fiat assets, and see real progress tied to those goals. Unlike a spreadsheet or a generic portfolio tracker, it combines goal framing, optional on-chain balance tracking, and manual contribution history in one workflow.
business_rationale: Reduce release risk by removing the largest unfinished and maintenance-heavy subsystems before public hardening.
Make the product easier to explain: create goals, add assets, track balances, record contributions, review progress.
Shorten QA scope so the remaining user journeys can be tested end to end on iOS and Android.
Preserve existing data contracts so the team does not pay migration complexity during the MVP cut.

This pruning decision is engineering-led and product-clarity-led rather than based on formal user research; launch KPIs, beta feedback tags, migration-banner dismissals, and support-article opens are the validation loop.
The retained shell, settings boundaries, and mutation surfaces stay compatible with future entitlement gating even though monetization decisions remain deferred.
problem: The current app contains multiple product directions at once: personal goal tracking, monthly planning, execution automation, reminders, family sharing, bridge sync, exports, and advanced dashboarding. That breadth increases startup work, navigation complexity, regression surface, and user confusion. The MVP release needs to feel intentionally small instead of partially disabled.
goals: Ship a minimal production-ready app centered on goal lifecycle, assets, balances, transactions, and dashboards.
Remove all non-core routes, background work, and settings so deleted features cannot be reached accidentally.
Keep SwiftData, CloudKit, and Android persistence contracts stable enough to avoid destructive migrations.
Define explicit UI states, acceptance criteria, and analytics so the MVP is a product spec rather than a deletion list.
Define a minimum viable visual system and mockup-grade screen specifications so MVP implementation is visually consistent across iOS, macOS, visionOS, and Android.
Lock the shipped platform scope, the root dashboard contract, and the release-gate retirement matrix so P4 is operational rather than aspirational.

Embed the minimum viable visual system, state-complete JSON-native mockup coverage, and a condensed implementation quick-reference directly in the proposal so UI delivery does not depend on a separate unwritten artifact.
Define explicit retirement contracts for cross-cutting notifications, root-shell state, and rollout gates so MVP pruning is implementation-safe rather than just file-targeted.
non_goals: Redesign CloudKit storage, SwiftData models, or Android Room entities for schema cleanliness.
Retain hidden access to removed production features behind obscure navigation or debug-only routes.
Ship family collaboration, reminders, monthly planning, forecasting, budget coaching, or export tooling in this release.
Introduce new net-new hardware integrations such as QR wallet scanning in the MVP release.

Bundle standalone Figma or illustration source files inside this artifact set; JSON-native mockup specifications remain the structural source of truth for this proposal pass, while visual comps are commissioned in parallel.
Ship localized production UI beyond English in the MVP release.
Define monetization or subscription strategy before MVP launch; revenue decisions are explicitly deferred to post-MVP roadmap planning.
Deliver a net-new visionOS-specific UX surface for the MVP release; the headset target remains compile-only parity in this proposal.

approaches_considered.name: Immediate hard deletion
approaches_considered.summary: Delete all non-MVP modules up front.
pros: Fastest way to shrink the codebase visibly.
Lowest chance of hidden navigation if the deletion is perfect.
cons: Too risky given current startup hooks, family-sharing runtime state, and dashboard contract coupling.
Likely to trigger long compile-fix loops before product behavior is stabilized.

approaches_considered.name: Feature flags only
approaches_considered.summary: Hide non-MVP functionality while leaving the full runtime graph intact.
cons: Leaves unsupported background work and stale routes in production.
Does not reduce conceptual or QA complexity enough for a stable MVP.

approaches_considered.name: Hybrid pruning with contract rewrites
approaches_considered.summary: Remove live entry points and background hooks first, version shared contracts where retained flows still matter, and only use short-lived flags for risky retirements.
pros: Matches the requirement to keep data as-is while still delivering a genuinely smaller app.
Creates an implementation path that addresses the actual iOS shell, family-sharing state, and dashboard parity contracts.
cons: Requires one explicit design pass before deletion starts.
Needs careful sequencing across iOS shell, Android navigation, and shared dashboard fixtures.

keep: Goal lifecycle: create, edit, archive/finish, delete.
Asset lifecycle: add, edit, remove, and assign assets to goals.
Crypto assets with optional blockchain address and chain selection for on-chain balance/transaction tracking via Tatum-backed services.
Goal balance, portfolio summary, progress indicators, a simplified root portfolio dashboard, and simplified per-goal dashboards.
SwiftData + CloudKit on iOS and current Android persistence contracts.
Debug/internal diagnostics needed to support on-chain troubleshooting and migration support.
UI test harness, reduced to MVP seed scenarios only.

Android asset allocation/edit-allocation route family, referred to in this proposal as ManageAssetAllocation; the current code file AssetSharingScreen.kt remains an implementation rename task, not a product-scope exception.
remove_from_production: Reminder notifications, monthly automation, and all notification settings.
Family sharing creation, invite acceptance, participant management, and shared-goal production surfaces.
Local bridge sync and QR import/export.
Forecast, heatmap, what-if, stacked, and other advanced dashboard visualizations.
Planning/budget copy catalogs and analytics tied only to removed systems.

explicit_module_audit.rationale: Current shortcuts are demo-level helpers and do not support the focused MVP loop.
The UI test bootstrap remains useful, but seed/reset branches must be reduced to goal, asset, transaction, and onboarding scenarios only.
The per-goal dashboard stays in MVP, but its contract must stop encoding planning and forecast semantics.
Only relevant to retired budget and planning guidance.
Only relevant to retired planning flows.
Audit after dashboard contract rewrite; if still tied to forecast/planning snapshots, retire it in Phase 3.
Analytics helper for removed planning commitments.
Copy catalog is planning-specific and should not survive the MVP cut.
Dashboard copy remains required but must align to actuals-based MVP semantics.

This proposal treats the retained surface as ManageAssetAllocation to match the single-user MVP. The current file/symbol name is implementation debt to rename during the pruning work, not a reason to delete the allocation workflow.
Dashboard v2 only becomes enforceable if validators, parity fixtures, and tests migrate with the new actuals-based contract.
Reminder fields remain in persisted models, but production goal creation, editing, onboarding, and restore flows must stop writing or scheduling reminders.

acceptance_criteria.happy_path: A user can create a goal with name, currency, target amount, start date, and target date; edit those fields later; archive/finish the goal; and delete it from the goal list.
acceptance_criteria.empty_states: If no goals exist, the dashboard and goals list present a single primary CTA to create the first goal.
acceptance_criteria.error_states: Validation blocks save when name, currency, amount, or dates are invalid and presents inline field errors.
acceptance_criteria.edge_cases: A legacy read-only shared record surfaces a non-destructive read-only message instead of edit controls.

acceptance_criteria.happy_path: A user can add a fiat or crypto asset to a goal, edit its currency/address/network settings, remove it, and manage allocations to the goal.
acceptance_criteria.empty_states: A goal with no assets shows an inline CTA to add the first asset from the goal dashboard and detail view.
acceptance_criteria.error_states: Invalid address format, missing network selection, or duplicate asset conflicts surface a clear blocking error before save.
acceptance_criteria.edge_cases: Fiat assets never require address or chain fields and remain fully manual.

acceptance_criteria.happy_path: For crypto assets, a user can optionally paste a wallet address, confirm or override the detected chain, save the asset, and see explicit tracking states: Connecting, Syncing, Connected, Stale, or Needs Attention, plus the last successful refresh timestamp and a manual refresh action.
acceptance_criteria.empty_states: If an asset has no address, the UI explains that manual transaction tracking still works and offers an add-address CTA.
acceptance_criteria.error_states: If Tatum or exchange-rate fetch fails, the last successful value remains visible with a stale/error badge, actionable copy, and retry from both asset detail and goal dashboard.
acceptance_criteria.edge_cases: If the user saves a crypto asset without an address, the asset remains valid but only manual transactions affect totals; if no successful balance has ever been fetched, the UI shows Waiting for first refresh rather than zero.

acceptance_criteria.happy_path: A user can add, edit, and review manual transactions and see the related goal total update immediately.
acceptance_criteria.empty_states: If a goal has assets but no transactions, the dashboard suggests recording the first contribution.
acceptance_criteria.error_states: Invalid amounts or asset mismatches block save with a field-level explanation.
acceptance_criteria.edge_cases: Adding a manual transaction must no longer trigger reminder rescheduling.

acceptance_criteria.happy_path: A user lands on a root portfolio dashboard as the default retained Apple entry surface and can open a per-goal dashboard that shows balance totals, progress, recent actuals trend, transaction activity, and practical next actions without forecast or planning clutter.
acceptance_criteria.empty_states: Root dashboard empty state is intentionally designed and points to goal creation; per-goal dashboards with missing assets or contributions point to the next concrete action.
acceptance_criteria.error_states: Loading, connecting, syncing, stale, and hard-error states are visually distinct and never silently show outdated data as current; recoverable failures always include retry and diagnostics/support affordances.
acceptance_criteria.edge_cases: Public MVP builds cannot expose custom widgets, forecast cards, or legacy visual fallbacks on the root dashboard, and the per-goal dashboard must ship on contract v2 only.

acceptance_criteria.happy_path: A new user finishes onboarding through a short first-session flow: welcome, optional first goal creation, recommended Add Asset next step, and then dashboard entry with one clear CTA.
acceptance_criteria.empty_states: If onboarding is skipped, the dashboard empty state still teaches the first action clearly.
acceptance_criteria.error_states: Onboarding can be exited safely without trapping the user in incomplete profile or template steps.
acceptance_criteria.edge_cases: Onboarding copy must not reference intelligent reminders, smart automation, or household sharing.

acceptance_criteria.happy_path: A user can change display currency, appearance, and access about/support information without seeing planning, family sharing, bridge, or export controls; Settings is always a retained root surface even though it is not a dashboard widget or planning hub.
acceptance_criteria.empty_states: If debug/internal diagnostics are unavailable in production, the diagnostics section is omitted entirely rather than disabled.
acceptance_criteria.error_states: If connectivity diagnostics fail, the status is displayed as unavailable instead of linking into removed feature surfaces.
acceptance_criteria.edge_cases: Android Settings remains a secondary route, not a bottom-nav tab.

acceptance_criteria.happy_path: On first launch after update, an existing user sees a concise one-time banner explaining the app has been streamlined to focus on goal tracking.
acceptance_criteria.empty_states: If the user has no remaining active goals after update, the dashboard uses the standard first-goal empty state plus a short note that old planning/share data is preserved but no longer surfaced.
acceptance_criteria.error_states: No destructive migration error is shown because feature retirement preserves storage contracts.
acceptance_criteria.edge_cases: Planning, reminder, and export data remains stored but hidden; the public MVP ship vehicle is the family-sharing transition release, so any incoming legacy share acceptance is intercepted fail-closed with guidance instead of reactivating sharing.

acceptance_criteria.happy_path: Goals list, asset list, and transaction history each support fast scanning, navigation into details, and one primary next-step CTA without exposing removed planning, sharing, or export features.
acceptance_criteria.empty_states: Each screen has a dedicated empty state with a single primary action: create goal, add asset, or record first transaction.
acceptance_criteria.error_states: Loading failures show inline recovery with retry and preserve cached content when available.
acceptance_criteria.edge_cases: Legacy reminder/share metadata never appears as an editable secondary section in these lists.

acceptance_criteria.happy_path: VoiceOver and TalkBack announce goal names, balances, progress, deadlines, stale/error state labels, and primary CTAs clearly on dashboard, goal detail, goal form, and transaction screens.
acceptance_criteria.empty_states: Dashboard and list empty states remain understandable at large dynamic type / large-font settings and do not rely on illustration alone to communicate the next step.
acceptance_criteria.error_states: Retry, dismiss, and support actions remain keyboard/switch-access reachable with sufficient contrast and spoken labels, and English-only MVP copy stays consistent across onboarding, migration, and error states.
acceptance_criteria.edge_cases: Critical amounts and deadlines do not truncate irrecoverably at large text sizes, and color is never the only indicator for stale, success, warning, or error states.

apple_root_contract.owner: ios/CryptoSavingsTracker/Views/ContentView.swift owns AppleRootShellState and is the single source of truth for retained root selection.

state_model.name: AppleRootShellState
state_model.persistence_rule: selectedRootSurface may persist across launches after onboarding, but first launch after onboarding completion or skip always seeds dashboard explicitly.

apple_root_contract.default_entry_rule: After onboarding or any migrated-user relaunch, AppleRootShellState.selectedRootSurface is seeded to dashboard. Goal Detail is never a retained shell root.

root_flow: CryptoSavingsTrackerApp presents OnboardingContentView only when onboarding has not been completed and no migrated data is available.
Onboarding completion or skip writes onboarding completion state, sets AppleRootShellState.postOnboardingDestination to dashboard, and hands off exactly once into ContentView.
ContentView renders Dashboard as the initial retained root surface on iPhone/iPad.
Selecting Goals switches AppleRootShellState.selectedRootSurface to goals; selecting a goal updates selectedGoalID and pushes the shared Goal Detail child flow.
Settings is opened by switching the same selectedRootSurface enum to settings rather than creating a separate planning-era root.
navigation_rules: MonthlyPlanningWidget and family-sharing sections are removed from every retained iOS host view before public MVP release.
Tapping a goal from Dashboard and Goals resolves through the same selectedGoalID child route.
No production iOS route lands directly on legacy planning, sharing, export, or bridge views.

root_flow: macOS keeps NavigationSplitView with sidebar items Dashboard, Goals, and Settings only.
selectedRootSurface drives the sidebar selection, and selectedGoalID drives the detail-column goal selection when the root surface is goals.
Dashboard is the default selectedRootSurface on first launch, after onboarding, and after migration-banner handoff.
If selectedGoalID becomes nil while the goals root is active, the detail column falls back to EmptyDetailView without surfacing MonthlyPlanningWidget or any retired controls.
Settings remains a first-class sidebar destination and the kept macOS Settings window points to the same stripped Settings contract.
navigation_rules: Goal Detail and Goal Dashboard stay inside the goals detail flow and never become extra sidebar roots.
Planning/sidebar entries, family access rows, local bridge sync, export, and reminder controls are removed from both sidebar and settings window.

visionos.shell_owner: Shared Apple SwiftUI shell must continue to compile against the same AppleRootShellState enum contract.
root_flow: visionOS is not treated as a customer-facing MVP release surface in this proposal because repo guidance states that platform capabilities exist but end-user UI is not yet implemented.
Shared dashboard/goal/settings views must still compile without removed planning, sharing, export, or reminder surfaces.
If a minimal shared-shell preview is exercised internally, it defaults to dashboard through the same selectedRootSurface enum but does not carry standalone release-gate obligations.
navigation_rules: No visionOS-only carve-out may preserve removed planner-era or sharing surfaces.
visionOS verification in this proposal is compile-only parity plus route-absence in shared code, not end-user release readiness.
Remove Screen.Planning, Screen.MonthlyPlanning, Screen.Execution, and Screen.PlanHistory from the production graph.
Keep Settings as a secondary route accessible from toolbar or overflow, not a bottom-nav root.
Use ManageAssetAllocation as the proposal name for the retained allocation workspace even if the initial code symbol still says AssetSharingScreen.

dashboard_empty_state.purpose: Make the first-launch state actionable and reassuring.
layout: Hero icon or illustration centered above copy.
Headline: "Track your first savings goal".
Body copy: "Create a goal, add an asset, and watch progress update as balances change."
Primary CTA: "Create Goal".
Secondary hint text: "You can add wallet addresses later for automatic crypto tracking."
wireframe: Create a goal, add an asset, and watch progress update as balances change.
You can add wallet addresses later for automatic crypto tracking.

dashboard_loading_state.purpose: Show honest progress while balances or exchange rates are loading.
layout: Skeleton summary card for portfolio total.
Two skeleton goal rows or cards.
Inline status label: "Updating balances…".
If cached data exists, keep it visible with a stale badge rather than replacing the entire screen with a spinner.

dashboard_populated_state.purpose: Make actual progress legible in one screen without forecast clutter.
layout: Section 1: Portfolio total and active-goal count.
Section 2: Active goals list ordered by nearest deadline, each with progress bar, current/target, and status badge.
Section 3: Simple recent balance trend chart.
Section 4: Lightweight CTA strip for add goal, add asset, or record contribution depending on state.
visual_hierarchy: Portfolio total is the largest number on screen.
Each goal row surfaces progress and deadline before secondary metadata.
Trend chart is supportive, not dominant.
Removed widgets are not replaced with decorative filler.
cta_priority_rules: If there are zero goals, primary CTA = Create Goal.
If goals exist and at least one goal has no assets, primary CTA = Add Asset.
If assets exist but no transactions and no successful on-chain refresh has occurred, primary CTA = Record Contribution.
If any active goal has stale balances, primary CTA = Refresh Balances.
Otherwise primary CTA = Record Contribution and secondary CTA = Add Goal.

goal_create_edit_flow.purpose: Reduce the form to fields required to define a goal and save confidently.
optional_fields: Existing visual customization that does not depend on planning or reminders
Short descriptive note if already supported by the model
interaction_rules: Primary save action remains visible at the bottom of the form.
Inline validation appears only after interaction or save attempt.
Edit flow keeps destructive actions grouped separately from core form fields.

settings_screen.purpose: Expose only core product preferences and support info.
sections: About: app version, privacy/support links, acknowledgements if already standard.
Diagnostics: hidden or debug/internal only; may show sync health and API status when enabled.
Update help: persistent "What changed in this update" row in About linking to the same migration support article for at least two post-MVP versions.

goals_list_states.loading: Show three goal-row skeleton cards with preserved top app bar and add-goal button shell.
goals_list_states.empty: Illustration + headline "Create your first goal" + one primary CTA.
goals_list_states.populated: Each row shows goal name, progress bar, current/target amount, deadline chip, and one status badge.
goals_list_states.error: Inline retry card above any cached rows; if no cached rows exist, show a centered error state with one retry button.

asset_list_states.loading: Show two asset cards with address/status placeholders.
asset_list_states.empty: Explain that assets fund goals and can be fiat or crypto; CTA = Add Asset.
asset_list_states.populated: Each asset row shows icon, currency, linked goal count, balance, and tracking badge.
asset_list_states.error: Failed refresh keeps last known balance visible with stale badge and retry affordance.

transaction_history_states.loading: Show one summary skeleton and four transaction row skeletons.
transaction_history_states.empty: State copy focuses on recording the first contribution rather than importing history.
transaction_history_states.populated: Rows show amount, asset, date, source label (manual/on-chain), and edit affordance only for editable records.
transaction_history_states.error: Inline error card preserves any cached list beneath it if available.

goal_detail_screen.purpose: Keep the retained goal workflow legible in one place: summary, assets, transactions, and dashboard entry without planning-era modules.
layout: Header card with goal name, current amount, target amount, deadline chip, and progress bar.
Primary actions row: Add Asset, Record Contribution, Open Dashboard.
Assets section with linked assets and tracking status badges.
Recent transactions section with the latest entries and a View All affordance.
No monthly-planning, sharing, reminder, or export sections.

full_page_error_state.purpose: Provide one recoverable hard-error design for network/API failures when no cached content can be shown.
layout: Centered warning icon above title and plain-language body copy.
Primary CTA: Retry.
Secondary CTA: Open Settings or Support only when the failing dependency is actionable by the user.
Optional footer line with last successful refresh timestamp when known.
wireframe: We kept your saved goals, but live data could not be updated right now.

migration_banner.purpose: Explain feature retirement without alarming existing users and give them one path to learn more.
layout: Inline dismissible banner at top of dashboard on first launch after update.
Short title + one sentence body copy + Learn More text action + Dismiss icon.
Learn More opens a support article describing retired features, preserved data, and the new MVP focus.
wireframe: CryptoSavingsTracker is now focused on personal goal tracking.
Planning, reminders, sharing, and export tools are no longer part of this version.

visual_contract.presentation: Inline top-of-dashboard banner, never modal in production.
visual_contract.layout: Title and body left-aligned; Learn More text action and dismiss affordance aligned trailing on larger widths or wrapped beneath on compact widths.
visual_contract.dismiss_behavior: Fade-and-collapse over 180ms, then permanently hide after dismissal or after the fallback teardown window elapses.

goal_dashboard_v2.purpose: Translate the actuals-only dashboard contract into one concrete layout before implementation.
layout: Header summary card with current amount, target amount, progress treatment, and deadline chip.
Next-action card immediately below the summary using one primary CTA and optional supporting copy.
Recent balance trend module with no forecast overlays or planner-era confidence labels.
Contribution activity list or empty state.
Allocation-health or asset-health card only when relevant to the current resolver state.
widget_rules: No custom widgets, forecast cards, or plan-this-month language may appear.
Exactly one primary CTA is visible at a time based on the resolver state.
On wide layouts, summary and next-action may sit side by side while trend/activity remain below.
responsive_behavior: Mobile uses a single vertical stack with summary, next-action, trend, activity, then health.
600-1023pt widths may place summary and next-action side by side only when both cards remain above the fold without truncating balance values.
1024pt and wider layouts may use a two-column first row and a balanced second row, but the primary CTA always remains inside the next-action card and trend/activity order stays unchanged.

state_specific_layouts.no_assets: Summary card + next-action card promoting Add Asset + supportive explainer card.
state_specific_layouts.no_transactions: Summary card + next-action card promoting Record Contribution + empty activity card.
state_specific_layouts.stale_balances: Summary card preserves last values, next-action promotes Refresh, and affected cards use stale tokens.
state_specific_layouts.hard_error: Summary card plus FeedbackStateSurface with Retry and optional diagnostics/support secondary action.

stale.user_message_pattern: Last updated <timestamp>. Pull to refresh or try again later.
stale.visual_rule: Warning token with preserved cached value and secondary-outline Refresh action.

needs_attention.user_message_pattern: Tracking needs attention: <reason>. Review details before balances can update again.
needs_attention.visual_rule: Error token with explicit reason text and Review Details primary action.

balance_trend_chart.purpose: Standardize the simplified actuals-only chart used on the root dashboard and goal dashboard v2.
balance_trend_chart.chart_type: Single-series line chart with optional soft area fill beneath the line on medium and wide layouts only.
balance_trend_chart.x_axis: Last 7 to 30 days depending on available data, labeled with concise dates and never with forecast intervals.
balance_trend_chart.y_axis: Currency-scaled balance values with a zero-baseline rule only when it does not flatten meaningful variation; otherwise use a padded min/max range and show the currency code in the axis title.
interaction: Default mobile state is static with the most recent point emphasized.
Tap/hover reveals a single-point tooltip with date and formatted balance.
If fewer than two data points exist, replace the chart with an explanatory empty-state card instead of drawing a misleading line.
state_rules: Stale data overlays a warning badge and last-updated timestamp without changing the underlying chart geometry.
Hard-error state swaps the chart module for FeedbackStateSurface rather than mixing chart chrome with error copy.
balance_trend_chart.accessibility: Expose a summarized spoken trend description plus the selected tooltip value when interaction is available.

named_error_scenarios.title: You are offline
named_error_scenarios.body: Saved goals and last known balances are still visible. Reconnect to refresh live balances and rates.
named_error_scenarios.secondary_action: Open Settings when the user needs connectivity help
named_error_scenarios.visual_treatment: warning tokens with preserved cached cards and last-success timestamp when available

named_error_scenarios.title: Wallet address looks invalid
named_error_scenarios.body: Check the pasted address and selected network before saving. Tracking cannot start until both match.
named_error_scenarios.visual_treatment: danger tokens with inline field error plus one summary card at the top of the form

named_error_scenarios.surface: asset tracking and diagnostics-aware refresh failures
named_error_scenarios.title: Live crypto data is temporarily unavailable
named_error_scenarios.body: We kept your saved balances and transactions, but the data provider could not complete a fresh sync right now.
named_error_scenarios.secondary_action: Open Support or internal diagnostics depending on build type
named_error_scenarios.visual_treatment: needsAttention tokens with explicit operator-facing reason hidden behind debug/internal affordance in production

named_error_scenarios.title: Balances need a refresh
named_error_scenarios.body: Your last successful update is still shown so you can keep tracking progress while live data catches up.
named_error_scenarios.visual_treatment: stale tokens with warning-soft border and preserved balance values

onboarding.headline: Welcome to CryptoSavings
onboarding.subheadline: Create savings goals, connect crypto or fiat assets, and track real progress without planning overhead.

feature_highlights.title: Goal Tracking
feature_highlights.description: Create personal savings goals and see current progress at a glance.

feature_highlights.title: Wallet Visibility
feature_highlights.description: Optionally add wallet addresses for automatic on-chain balance tracking.

feature_highlights.title: Manual Flexibility
feature_highlights.description: Record fiat accounts and manual contributions whenever balances move offline.

first_session_flow: Step 1: Welcome screen frames the product as personal goal tracking, not planning or automation.
Step 2: User chooses Create First Goal or Skip for Now; no asset, reminder, or sharing setup is forced up front.
Step 3: Create Goal form collects only name, display currency, target amount, start date, and target date.
Step 4: Post-save success state offers Add Asset as the recommended next step and Skip to Dashboard as the secondary action.
Step 5: If the user skips goal creation, the root dashboard empty state becomes the canonical first-value teaching surface.
interaction_rules: Onboarding uses at most two screens before the first goal form to minimize time-to-first-value.
Skip never strands the user in a dead end; it always lands on the empty dashboard with one clear primary CTA.
No onboarding step references smart reminders, family collaboration, budgeting, or export workflows.
If the user already has migrated data, onboarding is bypassed entirely and the migration banner takes precedence.
If onboarding enters a crypto setup path, the wallet-address teaching moment must appear as contextual education rather than a generic static hint.
onboarding.first_value_definition: First value is reached when the user sees a dashboard or goal dashboard containing at least one active goal with a non-zero current balance.

optional_mini_tutorial.availability: Shown only for brand-new users after the first goal is created and skipped entirely for migrated users.
optional_mini_tutorial.format: Two-step, skippable coach-mark/tutorial path: highlight Add Asset, then highlight Record Contribution or wait for the first balance refresh.
optional_mini_tutorial.goal: Accelerate time to first value without forcing a longer mandatory onboarding funnel.
optional_mini_tutorial.exit_rule: Dismiss forever after completion or explicit skip.

conditional_crypto_teaching_moment.trigger: User chooses a crypto asset or crypto-denominated goal during onboarding or immediately after first-goal creation.
conditional_crypto_teaching_moment.teaching_moment: Highlight the Wallet Address (optional) field and explain that adding an address enables automatic balance tracking while manual tracking remains available if the user skips it.
conditional_crypto_teaching_moment.dismissal_rule: Skippable in one tap and never blocks goal creation or dashboard entry.

first_run_coach_marks.scope: Launch-scope for brand-new users only; migrated users never see it.
steps: After the first goal is created, highlight Add Asset as the next recommended action.
After the first asset is linked or skipped, highlight Record Contribution or wait for the first balance refresh to explain how progress becomes visible.
interaction_rules: Each step is skippable in one tap.
Completion or skip dismisses the coach marks forever.
first_run_coach_marks.goal: Reduce setup friction and support the time-to-first-value KPI without creating a longer mandatory onboarding funnel.

steps.title: Choose asset type and currency
steps.detail: The user selects crypto or fiat. If fiat is selected, address fields disappear entirely.

steps.title: Enter wallet address (optional)
steps.detail: For crypto assets, the address field is optional and labeled as enabling automatic on-chain tracking.

steps.title: Confirm network
steps.detail: The app may auto-detect a chain from the asset symbol or address pattern, but the user can override it before saving.

steps.title: Run local validation before save
steps.detail: Validate required network selection and address format rules locally before creating the asset.

steps.title: Save and begin first refresh
steps.detail: On success, show immediate confirmation, move the asset into Connecting, then start the first balance/transaction refresh without blocking navigation.

steps.title: Show explicit tracking lifecycle
steps.detail: The asset and goal dashboard display stateful feedback: Connecting while the first request is queued, Syncing while balances/transactions are loading, Connected with last refresh timestamp after success, Stale when cached data is shown, and Needs Attention when the request fails with actionable guidance.

validation_rules: If a crypto address is present, a chain/network must be selected or auto-detected.
Address format validation runs locally using chain-specific rules before save.
The app never implies that an address is valid on-chain until the first successful balance fetch completes.

error_feedback.user_message: Invalid address format for the selected network. Check the address and try again.
error_feedback.presentation: Inline field error plus blocking save.

error_feedback.user_message: Balance data is temporarily unavailable. Your last saved data is still shown.
error_feedback.presentation: Non-blocking banner/snackbar plus stale badge on the asset/dashboard.

error_feedback.user_message: Fiat conversion could not be refreshed. Crypto balances remain visible.
error_feedback.presentation: Inline stale indicator near converted totals.

stale_balance_behavior: Retain and label the last successful balance/timestamp instead of zeroing the value.
Offer Refresh from the goal dashboard and asset detail.
If no successful balance has ever been fetched, show Waiting for first refresh rather than a misleading zero amount.
If the user is currently online and a refresh is running, keep the state at Syncing until either Connected or Needs Attention is known.
success_feedback: After save, show a toast/snackbar: "Asset added. Tracking will update shortly."
After a successful refresh, no modal is shown; the updated value and fresh timestamp are sufficient.

info_affordance.placement: Trailing info icon beside the "Wallet Address (optional)" label.
info_affordance.copy: Add a wallet address to refresh balances automatically. The app uses read-only blockchain queries and cannot move funds.
info_affordance.presentation: Bottom sheet on mobile, popover on larger screens, and accessible descriptive text for screen readers.

state_model.meaning: The asset was saved and the first on-chain request has not completed yet.
state_model.ui_treatment: Inline status row with progress indicator and neutral badge.
state_model.user_copy: Tracking is being connected for this asset.

state_model.meaning: A live refresh is in progress for balances and recent transactions.
state_model.ui_treatment: Preserve last known values if present, show syncing label, disable duplicate refresh taps.
state_model.user_copy: Updating balances and recent activity. Last known values remain visible.

state_model.meaning: At least one successful refresh has completed.
state_model.ui_treatment: Fresh badge plus last updated timestamp.
state_model.user_copy: Tracking is active and the latest values are current.

state_model.meaning: The latest refresh failed or exceeded freshness threshold but cached data exists.
state_model.ui_treatment: Warning badge, last successful timestamp, and user-initiated Refresh action.
state_model.user_copy: Live refresh is delayed. Your last saved values are still shown.
state_model.differentiation_rule: Stale always keeps the cached balance, shows the last successful timestamp, and presents Refresh as the primary action.

state_model.meaning: Validation, auth, rate-limit, or unsupported-chain failure blocks live tracking.
state_model.ui_treatment: Error badge, plain-language reason, and next-step guidance.
state_model.user_copy: Tracking cannot continue until the blocking issue is resolved.
state_model.differentiation_rule: Needs Attention always names a blocking reason and asks the user to review or fix it; it is never presented as a transient freshness delay.

usability_validation.timing: P2 prototype check and P3 implementation validation.
usability_validation.focus: Add-crypto-asset flow, especially address optionality, network confirmation, and state comprehension for Stale vs. Needs Attention.
usability_validation.participants: Target users matching the personal crypto saver persona rather than power users only.
success_criteria: Users can explain the difference between Stale and Needs Attention without prompting.
Users understand that wallet addresses are optional and read-only.
Users can recover from one blocking tracking state without facilitator intervention.

feedback_patterns: Use brief toast/snackbar confirmation after adding a goal, adding an asset, saving edits, and recording a manual transaction.
Use skeletons and inline status text for loading, not full-screen spinners unless the initial load has no cached content.
Use stale badges and retry affordances when external data is delayed; do not silently substitute old data as fresh.
Use non-blocking dismissible migration banner on first launch after update for existing users.
Use one shared full-page error pattern for hard failures: icon, plain-language title, recovery copy, primary Retry, and secondary Settings/Support only when relevant.
Inline field errors appear directly under the affected control; cross-field validation errors appear once at the top of the form and once inline near the blocking field.

All loading, empty, stale, and hard-error states use one reusable FeedbackStateSurface specification with title, supporting copy, visual token set, optional timestamp, and primary/secondary actions so MVP screens do not invent ad hoc variants.
All empty/loading/stale/error states follow one universal copy contract: explain the state plainly, preserve cached context when available, and present exactly one primary next action.

Named error contracts ERR-NETWORK-OFFLINE-01, ERR-WALLET-ADDRESS-INVALID-01, ERR-API-KEY-DEPLETED-01, and ERR-STALE-DATA-01 are mandatory shared treatments; retained screens may not invent generic fallback copy when one of these scenarios applies.

design_system.delivery_format: JSON-native high-fidelity mockup and token specifications; this artifact is the final visual source of truth for implementation.

principles: Use bright neutral surfaces with restrained teal/blue accents so financial status feels stable rather than speculative.
Keep the largest visual emphasis on progress, balances, and next steps; decorative elements must stay secondary.
Prefer card-based grouping with clear borders and soft elevation instead of heavy shadows or glossy crypto styling.

iconography.style: outline-first system icons with filled variants only for status emphasis
rules: Use one icon per card header or feedback message maximum.
Status icons may use success/warning/danger fills but must always pair with text labels.
Wallet/address info affordances use the neutral info icon, not warning styling.

p0_required_deliverables: Mini design system covering typography, palette, spacing, iconography, and interaction states.
Reusable FeedbackStateSurface spec for loading, empty, stale, and error states.
High-fidelity JSON-native mockups for dashboard, goal detail, onboarding, migration banner, and address-tracking states.

stale.meaning: Cached data exists but freshness has lapsed or the latest refresh failed.
stale.copy_rule: Always preserve the last successful timestamp and reassure the user that saved data is still visible.

needsAttention.meaning: Live tracking cannot continue until the user or operator resolves a blocking issue such as invalid input, unsupported chain, auth, or rate limiting.
needsAttention.copy_rule: State the problem plainly and name the next action; never present this state as a passive refresh delay.

connected.meaning: At least one successful refresh completed recently.

illustration_and_iconography.style_direction: Calm, finance-trustworthy, lightly geometric line-plus-soft-fill illustrations with restrained motion and no speculative crypto futurism.
icon_rules: Use rounded-outline symbols for neutral actions and states, with filled status glyphs reserved for success/warning/error chips.
Hero artwork stays simple enough to remain legible at 120-160pt on mobile and to degrade gracefully when removed for accessibility or compact layouts.
Wallet, refresh, alert, goal, and contribution metaphors must share stroke weight and corner radius tokens with the rest of the design system.
illustration_and_iconography.empty_state_art_direction: Use one focal illustration cluster per empty state rather than multiple decorative accents; the illustration supports the headline and never replaces explanatory copy.

crypto_logo_strategy.source: Use asset logos only for well-known supported currencies already present in the app asset catalog; otherwise fall back to a neutral two-letter monogram chip in the same radius and stroke system.
crypto_logo_strategy.fallback_rule: No third-party logo lookup is required for MVP. Unknown assets must still render cleanly without broken-image placeholders.
crypto_logo_strategy.contrast_rule: All logo or monogram chips sit on surfaceSecondary or surfaceAccent backgrounds and must preserve legibility in large-text and high-contrast modes.

primary.usage: Create Goal, Save Goal, Record Contribution, Retry

inputs.optionalInfoAffordance: Wallet address label shows an info icon that opens copy about automatic tracking and read-only security.

feedback_patterns.skeletons: Rounded shimmer blocks using skeletonBase/skeletonHighlight, matching final layout proportions.

feedback_state_surface.purpose: One reusable component contract for loading, empty, stale, and error states across dashboard, lists, and asset tracking surfaces.
rules: Loading uses skeletons plus inline status copy, not a blocking full-screen spinner when cached content exists.
Stale always shows the last successful timestamp when one exists.
Error always uses plain-language recovery copy and a Retry primary action when retry is possible.
The same component contract is used on iOS, macOS, visionOS, and Android with platform-native styling only.

onboarding_intro.background: backgroundCanvas with top-right radial wash using brandSecondary at 8% opacity
layout: Top 20%: compact logo/wordmark lockup and progress dots.
Middle 45%: layered card illustration showing one goal card, one wallet card, and one progress ring.
Lower 35%: headline, subheadline, three short feature rows, primary CTA, secondary skip link.
visual_notes: Headline uses hero token in textPrimary.
Feature rows each use one outline icon inside a 40x40 surfaceAccent circle.
Primary CTA spans full width with brandPrimary fill and no secondary animation beyond subtle press state.

dashboard_states.heroTreatment: single centered illustration made from a flag icon, asset chip, and ring chart line art
dashboard_states.cardSpec: none until user creates a goal
dashboard_states.secondaryCopy: Wallet addresses can be added later for automatic tracking.

dashboard_states.cardSpec: summary card plus two goal-row skeleton cards with top shimmer strips
dashboard_states.statusTreatment: small inline stale badge if cached values are shown while refresh is pending

dashboard_states.cardSpec: one summary card, stacked goal cards, one trend card, one CTA strip card
visual_notes: Portfolio total uses hero typography and brandPrimary only as accent, not full-bleed background.
Goal rows show progress bar, deadline chip, and one status badge aligned to the right edge.
Trend card uses chartLinePrimary with muted gridlines and no forecast overlays.

dashboard_states.cardSpec: same as populated state but stale elements gain warning badges and a thin warning border on affected cards
dashboard_states.statusTreatment: retry button is secondary-outline, not destructive

dashboard_states.cardSpec: one warning card with diagnostics CTA and last-successful-refresh timestamp if available
dashboard_states.statusTreatment: danger icon plus neutral explanatory copy; no red full-screen takeover

layout: Sticky top app bar with title and save action.
Single scroll column of surfacePrimary cards: basic info, timeline, optional customization, destructive actions only on edit.
Inputs vertically stacked with 12pt gaps and inline helper/error lines.
visual_notes: No reminder section is shown in MVP.
Target amount and currency form a split row with aligned baselines.
Date fields use outline buttons with calendar icon trailing adornment.

layout: Grouped list cards for Preferences and About.
Each row uses 72pt minimum height with right chevron only when navigable.
Diagnostics section is absent in release mockup and shown only in internal mockup variants.
visual_notes: Version row is visually quiet and not promoted as an interaction unless internal build is active.
No CSV, sharing, or planning controls appear.

layout: Top summary card with progress ring/bar, current vs target amount, and deadline chip.
Action row with Add Asset, Record Contribution, and Dashboard entry.
Asset cards with freshness badges and manual/on-chain labels.
Recent transaction list card with lightweight edit affordance.
visual_notes: Goal summary remains visually heavier than asset rows.
No planning widgets or forecast language appear in the detail surface.
Status badges use the same stale/manual/success palette as dashboard cards.

layout: Centered icon, title, body copy, retry CTA, and optional settings/support CTA.
Safe-area aware bottom spacing so buttons stay reachable at large text sizes.
visual_notes: Use warning/danger accent on icon only; the page background remains neutral.
Retry is always the primary button and uses the same filled treatment as other primary actions.

layout: Compact banner above dashboard content with title, one-line body copy, Learn More text action, and dismiss icon.
Banner collapses permanently after dismiss or after the fallback teardown window elapses.
visual_notes: Use warning-soft surface color rather than a blocking modal treatment.
Learn More is text-first so the banner stays compact and non-disruptive.
Banner height remains compact (roughly 72-88pt on mobile) and collapses with a short fade/height animation after dismiss.
The dismiss affordance must remain reachable at large text sizes without truncating the core retirement message.

goal_dashboard_v2.viewport: 390x844 mobile portrait plus wide responsive companion
layout: Top summary card with progress treatment, current vs target amount, and deadline chip.
Next-action card directly beneath summary using emphasis styling when user action is required.
Trend card with actuals-only line chart and compact legend.
Contribution activity card or empty-state card.
Conditional allocation/asset health card when relevant.
responsive_behavior: Wide: summary and next-action can share the first row; trend/activity occupy full-width below or a balanced two-column second row.
visual_notes: No forecast overlays, confidence meters, or planner-era comparative copy.
Primary CTA stays inside the next-action card rather than floating independently.
State badges match the shared status semantics tokens.

layout: Asset row preserves balance and last updated timestamp.
Warning badge with clock-refresh icon.
Secondary Refresh action aligned trailing.
visual_notes: Cached amount remains prominent.
Warning treatment is present but not destructive.
Explainer copy reassures that saved data is still visible.

layout: Asset row or card shows reason text under the asset name.
Error badge with alert icon.
Primary Review Details action plus optional Retry only if technically appropriate.
visual_notes: Blocking issue is named explicitly, not hidden behind a generic failure label.
Color and icon treatment are more severe than Stale.
The card does not imply that a passive refresh will solve the issue.

screen_reader_commitments: Goal rows, progress bars, balance cards, stale/error badges, and CTA strips expose explicit accessibility labels and values rather than visual-only context.
Migration banner, retry cards, and destructive actions expose clear spoken labels and dismiss/confirm hints on both Apple platforms and Android.
Wallet-address tracking states announce whether the value is fresh, stale, manual-only, or failed.
dynamic_type_and_large_text: Critical finance copy, progress amounts, and deadlines must remain readable at the largest supported dynamic-type / large-font settings used in release QA.
Primary actions reflow vertically when needed rather than clipping or truncating financial values.
Goal list rows and dashboard cards prefer multi-line metadata over compressed illegible layouts.
contrast_and_touch_targets: Text contrast meets WCAG AA 4.5:1 and non-text indicators meet 3:1 for status chips, stale badges, and progress treatments.
Interactive targets meet at least 44x44pt on Apple platforms and 48x48dp on Android.
release_checks: Run VisualRuntimeAccessibilityUITests plus one manual VoiceOver/TalkBack smoke on dashboard, goal detail, add/edit goal, and settings.
Verify migration banner, empty states, and full-page error states with large text and reduced-motion-safe transitions.
Block release if any primary goal/asset/transaction path depends on color alone or hides critical values from assistive technologies.

principles: Motion reinforces hierarchy and state change; it never obscures balances, deadlines, or primary actions.
Removed-feature replacements use calm, short transitions rather than celebratory crypto styling.
Every state transition must preserve cached data when available instead of flashing to blank containers.
screen_transitions: Dashboard to Goal Detail uses the platform-default push or split transition with no custom 3D or spring exaggeration.
Modal goal and transaction forms use a standard sheet transition with 180ms completion feedback only.
Root-surface changes between Dashboard, Goals, and Settings on Apple platforms use the platform container transition; no custom tab animation is introduced.
component_motion: Migration banner dismisses with a 180ms fade-and-collapse animation.
FeedbackStateSurface transitions between loading, stale, error, and content with 120-180ms opacity/position changes so cached data does not flash.
Progress bars and BALANCE-TREND-01 animate only on first appearance or meaningful value change, capped at 240ms.
CTA-strip button swaps crossfade over 120ms so the primary action changes cleanly when state changes.
motion_and_transitions.haptic_rule: Use light success haptics only for goal save and transaction save on supported mobile devices; error states rely on visual/text feedback only.
motion_and_transitions.accessibility_rule: Respect reduced-motion settings by falling back to fades with no scale or spring effects.

visual_delivery_contract.authoritative_statement: This proposal artifact now contains the authoritative final visual contract for MVP implementation: palette, typography, spacing, iconography, motion, named error states, and high-fidelity mockups are implementation-ready. External Figma or design-tool comps translate and verify this contract but do not replace it.
visual_delivery_contract.p0_review_gate: P0 closes only after product design, product owner, and platform leads approve the visual tokens, state matrix, and named error treatments in this proposal.
visual_delivery_contract.implementation_rule: Retained-surface implementation may proceed only against the visual rules defined here. If external comps diverge, the proposal must be revised; design drift is not resolved informally in implementation.
responsive_behavior: Mobile layouts are the canonical source; macOS and larger widths preserve the same hierarchy with additional horizontal space rather than new feature modules.
Dashboard and goal-dashboard cards may expand into two columns on wide layouts, but status order, CTA priority, and stale/error semantics stay identical.

translation_track.role: Translation, screenshot preparation, and QA verification only.
translation_track.must_finish_by: Before public beta screenshot capture and before P2 exit is declared complete.
translation_track.owner_group: Product design lead with platform implementation review
required_outputs: Visual comps matching HF-DASH-POP-01, HF-GOAL-DASH-V2-01, HF-SETTINGS-01, HF-GOAL-DETAIL-01, HF-ONBOARDING-01, and HF-MIGRATION-01.
A token parity sheet proving palette, typography, spacing, iconography, and named error treatments match this JSON contract.
Chart visual verification for BALANCE-TREND-01 across compact and wide layouts.
review_gates: Translation output may not reintroduce removed widgets or planner-era affordances.
Any token or hierarchy mismatch triggers proposal revision rather than ad hoc implementation override.
If translation artifacts are late, engineering may continue retirement work, but no retained-surface UI work can exit P2 until token-parity review passes.

breakpoints.label: medium
breakpoints.rule: Allow paired summary/next-action or split-form rows when financial values remain fully visible.

breakpoints.label: wide
breakpoints.rule: Allow two-column dashboard/detail compositions without introducing new modules or changing CTA priority.

surface_rules.root_dashboard: Summary and goal list stay visually dominant; trend and CTA strip may sit in a secondary column only on wide layouts.
surface_rules.goal_dashboard_v2: Summary plus next-action may share the first row on medium/wide layouts; activity and health cards never jump above the next-action card.
surface_rules.goal_detail: Action row collapses vertically before balance figures or status badges are truncated.
surface_rules.settings: Grouped cards may expand in width but remain one reading column; About/help rows never split into multi-column grids.

shared_rules: Responsive behavior cannot resurrect retired widget slots or planner-era side panels.
Large-text accessibility rules override breakpoint ambitions whenever there is a conflict.

crypto_network_selection_microcopy.surface: Crypto asset add/edit flow when wallet tracking is enabled.
crypto_network_selection_microcopy.title: Confirm network
crypto_network_selection_microcopy.inline_helper: Choose the blockchain this wallet actually lives on. Balances cannot update correctly if the network is wrong.
info_sheet_sections: What a network is in plain language.
Why the same coin can exist on multiple networks.
How to verify the correct network before saving.
What happens if the wrong network is selected and how to fix it later.
crypto_network_selection_microcopy.testing_note: This microcopy and the stale-versus-needs-attention distinction are mandatory usability-test prompts for novice crypto users during P2/P3 validation.

ios: Keep SwiftData models and CloudKit schema unchanged for the MVP cut.
Removed feature records may remain stored but must no longer drive visible production behavior.
android: Keep Room entities and repositories stable unless a retirement is proven isolated and risk-free.
Stop writing reminder/planning/share feature values from production UI before considering deeper schema cleanup.

retire_or_neutralize_after_surface_lockd: Planning and execution views, view models, services, and settings.
NotificationManager production entry points and reminder configuration views.
Family sharing UI, services, caches, rollout wiring, acceptance coordinator, and app delegate hooks.
Local bridge sync views and controllers.
Forecast, heatmap, what-if, and other advanced chart modules.

deactivation_matrix.current_evidence: CryptoSavingsTrackerApp requests notification permission during startup for non-test runs.
deactivation_matrix.mvp_action: Remove the request and any notification category setup from production startup.

deactivation_matrix.current_evidence: CryptoSavingsTrackerApp performs execution backfill and calls AutomationScheduler.checkAndExecuteAutomation plus scheduleAutomationNotifications.
deactivation_matrix.mvp_action: Remove execution backfill and all automation checks from startup.

deactivation_matrix.current_evidence: AddTransactionView reschedules reminders for each allocated goal after writes.
deactivation_matrix.mvp_action: Delete this scheduling side effect so transactions only update balances and history.

deactivation_matrix.current_evidence: SettingsView links to MonthlyPlanningSettingsView and MonthlyPlanningSettings stores payment/reminder preferences.
deactivation_matrix.mvp_action: Remove the settings route from production UI and stop reading these preferences anywhere user-facing.

deactivation_matrix.current_evidence: CryptoSavingsTrackerApp.runUITestSeedIfNeeded supports family-share, local-bridge, planning, and stale-draft scenarios.
deactivation_matrix.mvp_action: Keep only goal, asset, transaction, onboarding, and dashboard seed paths.

deactivation_matrix.area: Duplicate retained Apple views that still host planning widgets
deactivation_matrix.current_evidence: GoalsListView.swift and Components/EmptyDetailView.swift both still render MonthlyPlanningWidget outside the primary Dashboard/Settings shell.
deactivation_matrix.mvp_action: Remove MonthlyPlanningWidget and any retired-settings affordances from these duplicate retained hosts, and include them in route-absence testing before public release.

reminder_cleanup_targets.why: Goal archive/finish/delete flows are live production writes and must not leave reminder cleanup half-retired.
reminder_cleanup_targets.mvp_action: Clear reminder-related fields/invariants and restrict notification behavior to cancellation-only cleanup.

reminder_cleanup_targets.why: Edit flow still shapes goal-write payloads and can accidentally preserve reminder-era behavior.
reminder_cleanup_targets.mvp_action: Remove reminder inputs from state and ensure save intents delegate into the mutation boundary without reminder side effects.

reminder_cleanup_targets.why: Transaction saves currently touch reminder scheduling side effects.
reminder_cleanup_targets.mvp_action: Limit post-save behavior to transaction persistence, balance refresh, and MVP-safe feedback only.

reminder_cleanup_targets.why: First-run creation paths must obey the same reminder-retirement invariant as goal edit/create flows.
reminder_cleanup_targets.mvp_action: Ensure onboarding-created goals never write reminder configuration or schedule notifications.

android_pruning_plan.corrected_navigation_fact: Settings is currently a secondary route in AppNavHost, not a bottom-navigation root.
retire_or_neutralize_after_surface_lockd: GoalReminderScheduler bindings and worker scheduling paths.
Any collaboration/share copy that survives after route removal.

deactivation_matrix.current_evidence: AppNavHost bottomNavItems contains Dashboard, Goals, Assets, Planning.
deactivation_matrix.mvp_action: Remove Planning from bottom navigation and from the production nav graph.

deactivation_matrix.current_evidence: AddGoalUseCase, UpdateGoalUseCase, and DeleteGoalUseCase depend on GoalReminderScheduler.
deactivation_matrix.mvp_action: Stop writing reminder values from the UI; cancel legacy reminder jobs on save/delete; remove reminder DI bindings once flows are clean.

deactivation_matrix.current_evidence: AutomationScheduler and AutomationCheckWorker still exist in production code.
deactivation_matrix.mvp_action: Retire worker registration and code paths in the MVP release.

deactivation_matrix.current_evidence: Android includes local API-key storage and diagnostics for Tatum/CoinGecko.
deactivation_matrix.mvp_action: Keep developer/internal controls only in non-production builds; production builds expose status, not raw key-entry flows.

deactivation_matrix.current_evidence: AppNavHost keeps Screen.AssetSharing plus dedicated allocation routes, and AssetSharingScreen is the live allocation-management UI.
deactivation_matrix.mvp_action: Preserve the full allocation/edit-allocation route family and rename proposal language around these routes by behavior, not file name.

allocation_surfaces_retained: Screen.AssetSharing, Screen.AllocationList, Screen.AddAllocation, Screen.EditAllocation remain in MVP scope.
android_pruning_plan.naming_guardrail: In all proposal and implementation tasks, refer to AssetSharingScreen as the current allocation workspace until the code is renamed. Do not classify it as collaboration/sharing functionality.

goal_dashboard_contract_v2.decision: Keep the per-goal dashboard, but version the contract from v1 to v2 so the MVP dashboard is actuals-based rather than planning-based.

versioning.migration_rule: v1 parity fixtures remain in source control for historical reference but are no longer the release gate for MVP builds.

module_changes.notes: Still shows current vs target amount, progress ratio, remaining amount, and days remaining.
Remove behind-schedule and plan-this-month semantics. CTAs are limited to add_asset, add_transaction, fix_allocations, refresh_balances, edit_goal, and view_history.
Replace forecast and confidence language with an actuals-based recent balance trend module.
Show recent manual/on-chain contribution activity without planning goals.
Retain because allocations remain in MVP, but remove any recommendation language that depends on planning targets.
Actions stay limited to MVP-safe operations.

implementation_targets: Any GoalDashboard scene model, codec, telemetry, and copy-catalog dependencies that assume v1 semantics.

ci_test_gate_migration.current_dependency: Hard-coded REQUIRED_FIXTURE_FILES point to goal_dashboard_parity.v1 and scene_model.v1 artifacts.
ci_test_gate_migration.migration_action: Version the validator to accept v2 files as the normative MVP gate and fail if v1-only fixtures remain the release source of truth.

ci_test_gate_migration.current_dependency: Fixture existence checks are pinned to v1 schema/parity/round-trip files.
ci_test_gate_migration.migration_action: Update the expected fixture list to v2 and add assertions for removed forecast semantics and new actuals-only statuses.

ci_test_gate_migration.current_dependency: Tests load v1 parity and scene fixtures.
ci_test_gate_migration.migration_action: Swap test fixtures to v2 and assert the v2 next-action contract IDs/status language.

ci_test_gate_migration.current_dependency: Contract constants still include behindSchedule/onTrack planner-era state IDs.
ci_test_gate_migration.migration_action: Replace planner-era resolver states and status-chip IDs with v2 actuals-based equivalents.

ci_test_gate_migration.current_dependency: Next actions still emit IDs such as open_forecast and continue_last_data.
ci_test_gate_migration.migration_action: Remove forecast-driven actions, add refresh_balances or add_transaction style actions, and keep diagnostics only for hard-error states.

ci_test_gate_migration.current_dependency: Copy catalogs still contain forecast-oriented text and diagnostics references tied to v1 semantics.
ci_test_gate_migration.migration_action: Rewrite copy keys to actuals-based language and retire keys that imply planning/forecast guidance.

ci_test_gate_migration.current_dependency: Screen handlers still branch on open_forecast/continue_last_data action IDs.
ci_test_gate_migration.migration_action: Update CTA handlers, stale-state UI, and diagnostics affordances to the v2 action surface.

ci_test_gate_migration.current_dependency: Telemetry context and release documents implicitly assume v1 resolver states and module names.
ci_test_gate_migration.migration_action: Version telemetry event properties and release-gate documentation at the same time as the fixture migration so QA validates one contract only.

goal_dashboard_contract_v2.sequencing_dependency: Do not begin dashboard/root-dashboard v2 release-gate migration until the Apple root-shell contract and scene cleanup are merged. The product must not carry duplicate root-dashboard ownership while v1 and v2 fixtures coexist.

principles: No new share creation, acceptance, refresh, or participant management in production.
Authoritative owner data remains intact; invitee-specific caches may be removed locally because they are helper state, not the source of truth.
Transition release is fail-closed by default: rollout defaults off, refresh pipelines disabled, acceptance-only seams allowed temporarily.
existing_state_handling: Owner-created goals, assets, and transactions remain available as normal personal records after the feature cut.
Invitee projection data is removed from primary production navigation and is not editable in MVP builds.
Planning/share-related data remains stored but hidden from standard production flows.
acceptance_hook_removal: Remove FamilyShareAppDelegate and any scene/app delegate share-acceptance wiring from CryptoSavingsTrackerApp after the first-release migration banner is in place.
Remove FamilyShareAcceptanceCoordinator from production dependency graphs once legacy banner logic no longer relies on it.
cache_teardown: Clear FamilyShare cache namespaces and dirty-state stores on first successful launch of the MVP release after the one-time migration banner is acknowledged.
Stop FamilyShareReconciliationBarrier import observation in production startup.
Fallback teardown trigger: if the migration banner is never dismissed, retire local invitee caches automatically after 3 successful launches or 7 days from first MVP launch, whichever comes first.
Read-only invitee caches are not a reason to delay the public MVP ship; they are allowed only during the transition release and are removed in the cleanup release once the boundary gate passes.
authorization_boundary_after_removal: Goal, asset, and transaction mutation services default to owner-local write access only.
If a legacy shared/invitee record is still encountered directly, the app surfaces read-only messaging rather than mutating it.
user_visible_behavior: Remove Family Access settings row and shared-goals sections from the main UI.
Show one dismissible banner on first launch after update: the app now focuses on personal goal tracking; shared-goal collaboration has been retired in this version.
The migration banner includes a Learn More action that opens a support article with feature-retirement details and preserved-data guidance.

fail_closed_transition_release.ship_decision: This is the public MVP release vehicle.
runtime_rules: Remove all production family-sharing entry points from ContentView.swift, SettingsView.swift, and any other retained MVP route before cutting the public MVP candidate.
Production rollout defaults to sharing disabled; retained screens cannot call share creation, management, refresh, or participant actions even if legacy data exists.
Keep FamilyShareAppDelegate and any scene acceptance bridge only as fail-closed interceptors during the transition release.
Intercept connectionOptions and userDidAcceptCloudKitShareWith, record migration telemetry, show guidance, and return without reactivating sharing flows.
scenePhase changes, pull-to-refresh, and onAppear handlers must never trigger FamilyShare refresh or reconciliation work in MVP builds.
Owner-local personal goals remain editable; invitee/shared-goal data is hidden from primary navigation or shown read-only if encountered during transition cleanup.
release_gate_checks: No public navigation route exposes share creation, share management, or invitee mutation.
Attempted legacy share acceptance is fail-closed, non-crashing, and emits telemetry.
Migration banner and support article remain the only user-facing family-sharing communication in the transition release.
No retained MVP route calls familyShareCoordinator.refreshAllState or equivalent share refresh APIs.

release_boundary_gate.name: family_share_transition_to_cleanup_gate

deletion_scope: Delete FamilyShareAppDelegate production wiring from CryptoSavingsTrackerApp.swift.

cleanup_release.post_cleanup_rule: After cleanup, no production binary contains active family-sharing acceptance, cache refresh, or participant-management code paths.

transition_release_runtime_shutdown_plan.default_state: Production rollout defaults to sharing disabled on first MVP launch; no retained surface may call share creation, participant management, or invitee refresh APIs.

runtime_seams.transition_release_action: Keep only the minimum coordinator state needed for fail-closed acceptance messaging and migration telemetry. Remove production ownership of sharing refresh, owner actions, and participant-management flows from retained surfaces.

runtime_seams.release_invariant: The app launches, navigates, and refreshes MVP surfaces without invoking sharing state refresh.

runtime_seams.transition_release_action: Keep delegate/scene hooks only as interceptors that capture the incoming handoff, emit telemetry, surface migration guidance, and return without restoring sharing flows or share-state refresh.

runtime_seams.release_invariant: Legacy share acceptance is non-crashing, fail-closed, and cannot reactivate collaboration UI or background refresh.

reminder_retirement_contract.principle: Reminder data remains in storage for compatibility, but one authoritative write-boundary invariant owns retirement: every production persistence path clears reminder fields before save, and every lifecycle path is cancellation-only for legacy notifications.

ios_write_paths.current_behavior: Goal.init defaults reminderFrequency to weekly and reminderTime to 09:00 local time.
ios_write_paths.mvp_action: Change production goal-creation call sites to initialize reminder fields as nil; keep model properties only for legacy data compatibility.
ios_write_paths.legacy_handling: Existing goals may still contain reminder fields, but those values are not surfaced or re-scheduled.

ios_write_paths.current_behavior: Add Goal form defaults reminders on and writes reminderFrequency/reminderTime/firstReminderDate before save.
ios_write_paths.mvp_action: Remove reminder controls from the production form and stop writing reminder fields during goal creation.
ios_write_paths.legacy_handling: Preview/test-only reminder controls may remain outside the production navigation graph until deleted.

ios_write_paths.current_behavior: Edit flow can enable reminders, mutate reminder fields, and schedule/cancel notifications during save.
ios_write_paths.mvp_action: Remove reminder controls, clear reminder fields on first save of an edited goal, and convert any reminder-related save logic to cancellation-only cleanup.
ios_write_paths.legacy_handling: Editing a legacy goal becomes the main passive cleanup moment for leftover reminder data.

ios_write_paths.current_behavior: createGoal and restoreGoal schedule notifications when reminderFrequency is non-nil.
ios_write_paths.mvp_action: strip reminder fields before persist/restore and always cancel any existing notifications for the goal instead of scheduling new ones.
ios_write_paths.legacy_handling: archive/restore/resume remain lifecycle operations only; they never revive reminders.

ios_write_paths.current_behavior: Non-beginner onboarding templates inject weekly reminders and schedule them after save.
ios_write_paths.mvp_action: Remove reminder injection entirely and update onboarding copy so no user segment receives reminder defaults.
ios_write_paths.legacy_handling: Template-created goals are stored without reminder state in MVP.

ios_write_paths.current_behavior: Manual transaction save reschedules reminders for affected goals.
ios_write_paths.mvp_action: Delete reminder rescheduling side effects; transaction writes update balances/history only.
ios_write_paths.legacy_handling: If a legacy scheduled notification exists, transaction save does not refresh it.

ios_write_paths.current_behavior: Startup requests notification permission and runs automation/notification setup for non-test runs.
ios_write_paths.mvp_action: Remove notification permission requests, category setup, execution backfill side effects, and automation scheduling from production startup.
ios_write_paths.legacy_handling: One-time startup cleanup cancels known goal notification identifiers if previously scheduled.

android_write_paths.current_behavior: Goal form exposes reminder toggle/frequency and defaults newly enabled reminders to weekly.
android_write_paths.mvp_action: Remove reminder UI from production goal forms and force reminderEnabled=false in default/edit state mapping.
android_write_paths.legacy_handling: Legacy reminder fields may remain in loaded goals until the next save clears them.

android_write_paths.current_behavior: Normalizes reminder defaults and schedules GoalReminderScheduler after insert.
android_write_paths.mvp_action: Stop supplying reminder values from the UI and convert the use case to cancel-or-noop semantics for reminder jobs on create.
android_write_paths.legacy_handling: Created goals persist with null reminder fields.

android_write_paths.current_behavior: Normalizes reminder defaults and schedules/cancels GoalReminderScheduler based on active status.
android_write_paths.mvp_action: Strip reminder fields on update, cancel any existing job for the goal, and remove schedule() from the active-goal path.
android_write_paths.legacy_handling: Editing any goal becomes a cleanup path for old reminder metadata.

android_write_paths.current_behavior: Cancels reminder jobs on goal deletion.
android_write_paths.mvp_action: Keep cancellation behavior as the only remaining reminder side effect.
android_write_paths.legacy_handling: Delete remains safe for legacy scheduled jobs.

android_write_paths.current_behavior: Onboarding marketing copy still advertises smart reminders.
android_write_paths.mvp_action: Replace reminder-oriented copy with goal tracking, wallet visibility, and manual flexibility messaging.
android_write_paths.legacy_handling: No reminder expectations are set for new users.

job_cancellation_semantics: First launch after update cancels all known scheduled goal reminders/workers using goal identifiers already present in storage.
Goal save, archive, restore, finish, and delete paths are cancellation-only for any legacy reminder jobs.
No production UI or background worker may create a new reminder job after the MVP cut.

analytics_operational_contract.adapter_contract: All product events go through an app-owned AnalyticsTracking adapter with FirebaseAnalyticsTracker and NoOpAnalyticsTracker implementations.
analytics_operational_contract.configuration_owner: Build configuration plus DIContainer wiring owns provider selection. Missing Firebase plist/config or disabled analytics build settings must select the no-op adapter automatically.
fail_open_rules: App startup, onboarding, and goal flows continue normally when analytics configuration is absent or initialization fails.
Event emission failures are logged for diagnostics but never block save, navigation, migration, or release-gate flows.
The MVP release cannot be delayed solely because Firebase procurement/configuration is incomplete; the no-op adapter is the fallback.
privacy_and_store_review: Event catalog remains limited to the proposal-defined activation, dashboard, asset-tracking, and migration events.

analytics_operational_contract.release_rule: Firebase is the preferred provider, not a hard MVP dependency.
existing_repo_sources: ios/CryptoSavingsTracker/Utilities/NavigationTelemetry.swift currently emits KPI-adjacent navigation events through AppLogNavigationTelemetryProvider.

migration_boundary_rules: Retained KPI events are defined once in the app-owned AnalyticsTracking adapter and forwarded to FirebaseAnalyticsTracker or NoOpAnalyticsTracker.
AppLog may remain for engineering diagnostics, but AppLog-only emissions do not count as product analytics and must not be used as a second KPI source of truth.
