analytics_operational_contract.kpi_event_authority: Only events emitted through the app-owned analytics adapter count toward activation, retention, funnel, and migration KPI reviews.

mutation_boundary_contract.principle: Production goal, asset, allocation, and transaction mutations flow through authoritative services/use cases only; view code collects input, triggers intents, and renders results.

Message copy: "CryptoSavingsTracker now focuses on personal goal tracking, assets, and balances. Planning, reminders, sharing, and export tools are no longer part of this version. Your existing goal and asset data is still preserved."
Provide a secondary Learn More link to a support article explaining retired features, preserved data, and where the main goal-tracking workflows now live.

If the user opens the app from a legacy family-share acceptance handoff during the public transition release, show the same migration explanation plus a Learn More path instead of reviving sharing flows.
Provide the same Learn More destination as a persistent Settings/About link labelled "What changed in this update" for at least the first two post-MVP versions.
release_notes: Update TestFlight/App Store/Play notes to describe the reduced scope explicitly.
Call out that planning, reminders, family sharing, local bridge sync, and export are retired in this version.
Link the support article from release notes where store policy and channel constraints allow.
data_visibility_rules: Goal, asset, transaction, and balance data remains visible and editable as normal if it belongs to the user.
Planning/execution/reminder/export records remain stored but are hidden from production UI.
Invitee family-share caches are retired locally after the migration banner is acknowledged; CloudKit authoritative data is not destructively deleted.
beta_cohort_communication: If a known TestFlight or beta cohort exists, send the same streamlined-scope message through release notes or direct communication before public rollout begins.
Use consistent wording across pre-update communication, migration banner copy, release notes, and the support article.

persistent_help_entry.location: Settings > About > What changed in this update
persistent_help_entry.availability_window: Keep visible for at least the first two post-MVP versions, then re-evaluate based on confusion/support volume.

platform_sequence: 1. Freeze the inline visual system, launch the parallel visual-design translation track, lock KPI/support artifacts, and record the baseline-document prerequisite before implementation starts.
2. Clean up Apple root-shell ownership and Scene/WindowGroup registration first so Dashboard-first navigation is authoritative before any dashboard-v2 widget or forecast removal begins.
3. Shut down reminder writes and family-sharing runtime seams second under one retirement coordinator, including migration-banner state, fallback teardown, and cache cleanup ownership.
4. Replace root dashboard and goal dashboard contracts with v2 layouts/states only after the shell and shutdown work land.
5. Bring Android to the same retained-product contract, then run cross-platform hardening and staged rollout monitoring.

phases.name: Design Freeze
deliverables: Approve this revised MVP scope, inline visual system, named error states, and state-coverage matrix.
Approve dashboard contract v2, migration messaging, and the cross-cutting retirement matrix.
Approve onboarding copy rewrite and first-run coach-mark spec.
Approve the support article outline, persistent Settings/About help entry, and proposal navigation guide.
Approve the one-page implementation quick-reference.
Confirm Firebase Analytics as the MVP event transport for both platforms.
Confirm the Android follow-up ship is no more than one sprint behind iOS if same-train landing is not feasible.
Approve the platform scope table for iOS, macOS, compile-only visionOS parity, and Android.
Approve the root portfolio-dashboard contract and CI retirement matrix.
Assign Product Owner as install-base baseline snapshot owner with a deadline of 2026-04-26.
Run one half-day competitive feature-mapping and user-demand snapshot session during P0.
Commission the visual translation track as token-parity verification only; it may not redefine approved visual rules.
Write the reusable current-system baseline artifact at .review-baselines/current-system-baseline.md before P1 coding starts.
phases.calendar_target: 2026-W15 (2026-04-06 to 2026-04-12) with a planned approval/contingency buffer on 2026-04-13 to 2026-04-14 before P1 is considered late.

phases.name: Surface Lockdown
deliverables: Make ContentView and CryptoSavingsTrackerApp the authoritative Apple root-shell and scene-registration cleanup layer before any dashboard-v2 migration begins.
Remove planning, sharing, bridge, export, and reminder entry points from navigation and settings.
Remove notification permission and automation startup hooks.
Insert first-launch migration banner for existing users.
Instantiate the RetiredFeatureTransitionCoordinator that owns migration-banner acknowledgement, first-launch timestamps, fallback teardown, and family-sharing cache purge.
Implement fail-closed family-share acceptance interception before production delegate/coordinator removal.
Delete the macOS Goal Comparison window registration from CryptoSavingsTrackerApp before the first public candidate.
Expand Apple route-absence cleanup to duplicate retained hosts such as GoalsListView and EmptyDetailView, not only the primary shell views.

phases.name: Core Flow Simplification
deliverables: Disable family-sharing refresh hooks, scenePhase-driven runtime work, and FamilyShareReconciliationBarrier startup observation.
Enforce reminder retirement across GoalLifecycleService, GoalEditViewModel, AddTransactionView, and OnboardingMutationService.
Simplify goal create/edit flows and onboarding copy.
Define and ship root dashboard and goal dashboard v2 UI states from the inline mockups.
Finalize crypto-address tracking feedback, including explicit Stale vs. Needs Attention visual/copy separation.
Extend goals/assets/transactions list states to the defined loading, empty, populated, and error designs.
P2 cannot close until the token-parity review confirms external translation artifacts match the proposal-defined visual contract.

phases.name: Dependency Retirement
deliverables: Retire family-sharing runtime hooks and local caches per the retirement contract.
Remove reminder scheduling and automation workers on both platforms.
Delete isolated leaf modules after compile paths are clean.
Delete any remaining transition-only family-share acceptance interceptors and coordinator wiring once the release-boundary gate passes.
Retire or debug-gate any non-shell Apple window/scene registrations that do not belong to the MVP contract.
Run moderated usability testing on the add-crypto-asset flow and feed findings back into the inline mockup/state specs before release hardening closes.
Use the named family-sharing transition artifact exit telemetry and automated tests to decide whether the cleanup release gate is ready to remove the last fail-closed bridge code.

phases.name: Hardening and Release Gate
deliverables: Run MVP build/test suites.
Verify no removed route is reachable.
Validate existing data loads without destructive migration.
Verify product and analytics instrumentation for launch KPIs and migration signals.
Execute the CI retirement matrix and confirm every removed-feature suite/runbook has been deleted, replaced, or explicitly retained with compatibility rationale.
Validate App Store / Play listing copy, release notes, Settings help link, and support article links for the streamlined MVP positioning.
Verify CryptoSavingsTrackerApp scene registration contains no production Goal Comparison window and no retired Apple entry point.
Attach install-base snapshot plus informal beta/TestFlight feedback themes to the release checklist before final go/no-go.

rollout_plan.target_release_window: Target ship window: 2026-Q2, with proposal approval on 2026-04-05, P0 beginning 2026-04-06, and a built-in two-day contingency buffer before P1 is considered delayed.
rollout_plan.platform_timing_decision: Ship iOS first only if needed for sequencing, but Android must follow on the same contract no more than one sprint later.

store_review_and_support_plan.risk: Removing visible capabilities from an existing listing can trigger reviewer questions and user confusion if the update is framed as a silent downgrade.
mitigations: Release notes and store descriptions frame the update as a focus decision: personal goal tracking, assets, balances, and dashboards are now the core product.
Publish one support article linked from the migration banner and Settings support row describing retired features, preserved data, and recommended next steps.
If reviewer notes are needed, explain that no data-loss migration occurs and that the update removes unfinished/unsupported product surfaces for reliability.
pre_update_communication: If an identifiable TestFlight or beta cohort exists, publish brief pre-update release notes or a short email describing the streamlined MVP focus before Wave 1 public rollout.
Keep the message aligned with the in-app migration banner and support article so users are not surprised by removed planning, sharing, reminder, or export surfaces.

re_enablement_framework.review_window: Review retired-feature demand at 30, 60, and 90 days after launch, then quarterly; the 30-day review explicitly includes migration-banner and support-article engagement signals.
entry_conditions: Core-loop completion and 30-day retention are at or above their agreed floors for one full review window.
A retired feature accounts for at least 20% of tagged post-launch support/feedback requests from retained users across two consecutive review windows.
The feature can be reintroduced without breaking the MVP mental model or forcing destructive persistence changes.
re_enablement_framework.default_rule: No retired feature returns during MVP hardening. Reintroduction is a later roadmap decision backed by evidence, not by isolated stakeholder preference.
qualitative_review_inputs: tagged support contacts about retired features
App Store / Play review complaints tied to removed surfaces
Lightweight competitive or user-demand snapshot captured before the first 30-day review
re_enablement_framework.synthesis_owner: Product owner produces the written recommendation memo for each 30/60/90-day and quarterly review, with platform-lead and design input.
re_enablement_framework.decision_artifact: Each review window ends with one written recommendation covering KPI movement, qualitative feedback themes, migration signals, competitive-demand snapshot results, and a yes/no recommendation on re-enablement.

pre_review_research.timing: Complete one lightweight competitive and user-demand snapshot before the first 30-day re-enablement review.
minimum_output: One synthesis of tagged user-demand/support signals
One short recommendation on whether the retained loop appears under- or over-scoped

staged_store_rollout.internal_validation: Complete TestFlight/internal dogfood and beta smoke before any public store exposure.
production_exposure_plan: Wave 1: use the smallest available App Store phased-release step and 10% Play rollout for the first 48 hours.
Wave 2: expand to approximately 50% exposure for the next 48 hours if crash-free sessions stay at or above 99.5% and no blocking migration regressions appear.
Wave 3: expand to 100% after roughly 96 hours total if health metrics, banner/support signals, and blocker review remain green.
pause_triggers: Any destructive-migration symptom or inaccessible root-shell regression is detected.
Tagged support or store-review volume indicates users are confusing hidden retired features with data loss.
Wave 1 migration confusion reaches material levels: retired-feature confusion accounts for at least 20% of inbound tagged feedback or support volume.

Migration-banner dismisses stay high while support-article opens and Settings help opens stay low and confusion reports rise, indicating users are dismissing guidance without understanding the scope change.
staged_store_rollout.store_capability_note: If a store only supports fixed phased-release steps, use the closest lower step that approximates the target exposure curve.

wave_1_signal_review.review_window: End of the first 48-hour Wave 1 window before expanding exposure.
wave_1_signal_review.default_action: Hold at the current exposure step until the product owner and release owner agree that confusion is understood and acceptable.

install_base_baseline_plan.current_context: This proposal stage has no trustworthy repo-local install-base snapshot.
install_base_baseline_plan.deadline: 2026-04-26 (end of P1) and no later than the P4 go/no-go gate.
install_base_baseline_plan.requirement_before_p4: Capture a baseline snapshot from App Store Connect, Play Console, and Firebase/Crash tooling covering installs, active users, retention cohort denominator, and active beta/TestFlight cohort size.
install_base_baseline_plan.working_assumption: Treat the pre-public denominator as beta/pre-launch scale until that snapshot is attached to the release checklist.
additional_release_inputs: Attach any informal beta/TestFlight feedback notes or tagged support observations that validate or challenge the MVP pruning rationale.
Record the active beta/TestFlight cohort size alongside install and retention denominators so rollout decisions have the right context.
Summarize recurring qualitative TestFlight themes so the re-enablement framework starts with a pre-launch sentiment baseline.

support_article_delivery.p0_gate: The support article outline is approved before implementation starts so the migration banner and Settings help row have a real destination.
customer_surfaces: Settings > About > What changed in this update
Release notes/store listing links where possible

android_parity_escalation.commitment: Android remains on the same retained-feature contract and should ship no more than one sprint behind iOS.
android_parity_escalation.trigger: Escalate if Android P2 is not code-complete within 5 business days of the corresponding iOS P2 merge, or if Android route-removal coverage drops below the proposal-defined MVP contract.
android_parity_escalation.default_decision_rule: Default to holding broader release claims until the owners explicitly choose between holding iOS rollout, narrowing exposure messaging, or decoupling timing with a written rationale.

p0_to_p1_buffer.rule: Use these two business days to absorb approval latency on the visual contract, current-system baseline ownership, and KPI baseline ownership without immediately slipping the full schedule.
p0_to_p1_buffer.escalation: If P0 approvals still remain open after 2026-04-14, treat the schedule as formally delayed and re-baseline P1-P4 rather than pretending the original dates still hold.

buffer_and_contingencies.visual_translation_delay_rule: If translation comps are late, engineering may continue non-visual retirement and shell-cleanup work, but retained-surface UI work may not exit P2 and no screenshot/release-marketing capture may begin until token-parity review passes.
buffer_and_contingencies.android_parity_rule: If Android falls more than five business days behind the committed one-sprint lag, escalate to product owner and both platform leads with an explicit ship/no-ship decision record.
buffer_and_contingencies.competitive_mapping_rule: The half-day competitive snapshot is not a hard blocker for P1 coding, but it must finish during P0 so the first 30-day re-enablement review is grounded in more than intuition.

funnels: Core loop: goal_created -> asset_added -> transaction_recorded or crypto_balance_fetch_succeeded -> dashboard_viewed.
analytics_plan.decision_support: These events are the minimum evidence base for deciding whether any retired capability deserves re-introduction later.

transport_decision.reasoning: It is the fastest cross-platform path to capture the defined MVP activation, retention, and funnel events on iOS and Android without building a custom backend first.
transport_decision.implementation_note: Wrap SDK calls behind a small app-level analytics adapter so event names/properties stay proposal-owned and can move later if the team changes providers; missing Firebase config falls back to a no-op adapter rather than blocking MVP release.

analytics_plan.implementation_phase: P0 decision, P1 integration, P4 KPI validation.

time_to_first_value_definition.emit_condition: Emit once per install on the first dashboard_viewed or goal_dashboard_viewed session where at least one active goal has a non-zero current balance.
time_to_first_value_definition.measurement_window: First launch timestamp to event timestamp.
time_to_first_value_definition.reasoning: This matches the proposal definition of first visible goal progress and avoids ambiguous proxies such as goal creation with zero balance.

channels: Support article opens and tagged support contacts tied to retired-feature questions.
App Store / Play review monitoring for regression complaints after the MVP cut.
Beta/TestFlight feedback tagged by retired feature area.
Migration banner seen/dismissed rate by platform.
Support article open volume and follow-on support contacts tied to retired-feature questions.
qualitative_signal_sources.usage: Feeds the re-enablement framework so retired-feature decisions are based on analytics plus structured qualitative signals, especially migration-banner behavior and support-article engagement during the first 30 days.

product_kpis.target: >= 65% of new users create their first goal within 3 days of install.
product_kpis.rationale: Set above generic utility-app activation floors because the MVP offers one dominant first-run job, but still treated as a hypothesis until the P4 denominator snapshot is attached.

product_kpis.target: >= 45% of activated users reach goal -> asset -> transaction/balance -> dashboard within 7 days.
response_bands: <25%: treat as a serious onboarding/product-friction issue; pause any scope re-expansion and run targeted activation research immediately.
25-35%: keep MVP scope fixed, focus the next cycle on onboarding, empty-state, and asset-linking friction reduction.
35-45%: acceptable but below target; improve copy, loading/error recovery, and CTA sequencing before considering new features.
product_kpis.rationale: Chosen as the clearest proof that users can move from setup into visible progress in a simplified product, not merely complete data entry.
Anchored to early consumer-finance/mobile-utility retention expectations for a niche tracker that should still create at least weekly return value.
Represents the minimum monthly habit signal needed before investing in broader scope or re-enabling retired features.
A pruning release must improve trust, so reliability remains a hard release-health gate rather than a soft aspiration.

product_kpis.target: <= 10 minutes from first launch to time_to_first_value_achieved.
product_kpis.rationale: The MVP is only defensible if the product reaches visible progress quickly; longer setup times would undermine the simplification thesis.

engineering_release_criteria: No production route or CTA reaches planning, reminders, sharing, bridge sync, export, or what-if flows.
iOS, macOS, visionOS shared-Apple-shell, and Android MVP-focused build/test suites pass for the surfaces declared in platform_scope.
Root portfolio dashboard and goal dashboard both ship exactly one MVP contract; no legacy/custom-widget/forecast runtime path survives the public release gate.
Dashboard v2 parity fixtures, validators, workflow gates, and release-gate docs replace v1 for MVP builds.
The CI retirement matrix has been executed and every removed-feature suite/runbook is deleted, replaced, or explicitly retained with compatibility rationale.
Startup no longer requests notification permissions or runs planning automation.
Screen reader labels, large-text layouts, contrast, and target-size checks pass for dashboard, goal detail, add/edit goal, and settings.
Existing goal and asset data load without destructive schema migration.
CryptoSavingsTrackerApp production scene registration contains no Goal Comparison window group or other retired Apple entry point.

Family-sharing transition seams are neutralized: no retained surface triggers refreshAllState, no production startup starts FamilyShare reconciliation/refresh drivers, and scenePhase changes cannot revive sharing runtime work.
success_metrics.interpretation: All KPI targets are initial launch hypotheses, not hard product gates. Re-baseline them after the first 30 days of cohort data.
success_metrics.review_cadence: Review KPI thresholds weekly during staged rollout, at 30 days after launch, and then monthly for the first quarter.

time_to_first_value_definition.emit_condition: Emit once per install on the first dashboard_viewed or goal_dashboard_viewed session where at least one active goal has a non-zero current balance.
time_to_first_value_definition.measurement_window: First launch timestamp to event timestamp.
time_to_first_value_definition.reasoning: This matches the proposal definition of first visible goal progress and avoids ambiguous proxies such as goal creation with zero balance.

baseline_context.current_install_base: TBD until Product Owner captures the console snapshot by 2026-04-26.
baseline_context.current_engagement_baseline: TBD until the same 2026-04-26 snapshot is attached.
baseline_context.reason: The proposal should not invent denominators; KPI interpretation attaches to an explicit baseline export before public rollout.

kpi_rationale.summary: These thresholds are launch hypotheses based on the simplified single-user MVP funnel and broad mobile utility/consumer-finance benchmark ranges, not on a trustworthy pre-existing repo-local baseline. They are strong enough to detect whether simplification helped, while still explicitly subject to re-baselining after the first 30 days of real cohort data.
benchmark_basis: Activation is expected to improve because onboarding now centers on one job: create a goal and reach a dashboard with progress.
Retention targets are intentionally moderate for a niche savings tracker and are used as signal thresholds, not promises.
Core-loop completion is treated as the most product-specific metric because it measures the retained product story end to end.

migration_signals.purpose: These are operational rollout signals, not vanity metrics. They exist to detect whether existing users interpret the MVP cut as focus or as unexplained loss.

qualitative_feedback_baseline.capture_point: P4 install-base snapshot and each staged-rollout review.
inputs: Tagged support conversations about removed features
App Store / Play review excerpts related to confusion or praise about the simplified scope
Lightweight competitive or user-demand snapshot prepared before the first 30-day re-enablement review.
qualitative_feedback_baseline.usage: Populate the re-enablement framework with a non-zero qualitative baseline before the first public 30-day review.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Live iOS shell still exposes planning or sharing through surfaces that were not part of the original proposal.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Start with ContentView, MonthlyPlanningWidget, DetailContainerView, SettingsView, and app startup rather than coordinator-only cleanup.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Family-sharing retirement breaks owner or invitee edge cases.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Use the explicit retirement contract, keep owner-local records editable, and make any remaining legacy shared state read-only or hidden rather than mutating it blindly.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Dashboard runtime still depends on planning-era parity contracts.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Version the dashboard contract to v2 before deleting forecast/planning modules.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: External API failures make crypto tracking feel unreliable.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Preserve last successful data, show stale/error states clearly, and keep manual transactions available as a fallback.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Feature-flag sprawl leaves pruning work half-finished.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Hard-delete obvious removals and keep only three short-lived flags with a one-release sunset rule.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: App Store / Play reviewers or existing users interpret the streamlined release as a silent downgrade.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Frame the release as a reliability-and-focus update in store copy, release notes, migration banner copy, and the linked support article.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Apple-platform drift leaves macOS or visionOS surfaces carrying removed planning/sharing controls because they share less-exercised shells.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Use the platform_scope table as a release gate and require route-absence smoke on iOS stack, macOS split view, and the shared Apple shell.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Firebase setup or privacy review slips and threatens the MVP schedule.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Use the analytics operational contract with a no-op fallback so measurement degrades gracefully instead of blocking release.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Family-sharing runtime seams survive via scenePhase forwarding, startup observation, or retained-surface refresh hooks after the visible UI is removed.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Disable rollout by default, remove refreshAllState from retained surfaces, stop FamilyShareReconciliationBarrier and related refresh drivers at startup, and restrict any surviving delegate hooks to fail-closed acceptance interception only.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: macOS production builds still expose removed functionality through the separate Goal Comparison window group.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Audit all WindowGroup registrations in CryptoSavingsTrackerApp.swift, delete Goal Comparison before the public candidate, and make route-absence validation fail if it remains.

resolved_open_questions.question: Should forecast insights survive on the goal dashboard?
resolved_open_questions.decision: No. Forecast semantics are removed entirely in MVP and replaced with actuals-based balance trend in dashboard contract v2.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: The MVP value proposition is tracking actual progress, not projecting future outcomes.

resolved_open_questions.question: Should Android developer API-key/cache controls remain visible?
resolved_open_questions.decision: Only in debug/internal builds, not in the production UX.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: On-chain diagnostics may still matter to the team, but raw developer controls are not part of the customer-facing MVP.

resolved_open_questions.question: Should CloudKit sync health be user-facing?
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: The primary product promise is progress tracking, not sync management. Expose more only if support data proves it is necessary.

resolved_open_questions.question: Which analytics SDK will collect the MVP events and funnels?
resolved_open_questions.decision: Firebase Analytics wrapped behind a thin app-level adapter.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: The repo has no product analytics backend today, and Firebase is the fastest cross-platform route to make the measurement plan implementable.

resolved_open_questions.question: How should debug/internal diagnostics be gated?
resolved_open_questions.decision: iOS uses Debug-configuration-only diagnostics and Android uses debug buildType plus the existing developer reveal gesture; production builds expose status only.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: This keeps troubleshooting tools available to the team without leaking raw key-entry or diagnostics flows into customer-facing builds.

resolved_open_questions.question: What is the Android timing decision for MVP launch?
resolved_open_questions.decision: iOS may lead sequencing, but Android must ship on the same contract within one sprint.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: That limits parity drift while preserving the practical need to land the higher-risk iOS retirement paths first.

resolved_open_questions.question: What is the explicit MVP platform scope across Apple platforms and Android?
resolved_open_questions.decision: iOS, macOS, and Android are customer-facing MVP release surfaces. visionOS remains compile-only parity for shared-shell cleanup because repo guidance says UI is not yet implemented there.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: This resolves the repo inconsistency by keeping shared Apple code honest without pretending there is a release-ready headset UI contract.

resolved_open_questions.question: What is the localization scope for the MVP release?
resolved_open_questions.decision: English-only UI and release communications for MVP, with copy catalogs kept ready for future localization.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: The immediate release risk is product-surface simplification, not multi-language expansion.

resolved_open_questions.question: How is time-to-first-value measured without ambiguity?
resolved_open_questions.decision: By a dedicated time_to_first_value_achieved event emitted on the first dashboard session that shows at least one active goal with a non-zero balance.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: This measures visible progress rather than mere setup completion.

resolved_open_questions.question: What owns the Apple root dashboard route in MVP?
resolved_open_questions.decision: ContentView owns a single AppleRootShellState model with selectedRootSurface, selectedGoalID, activeSheet, and postOnboardingDestination; it defaults to Dashboard after onboarding on iOS and macOS, while visionOS only needs compile-safe parity with the same enum contract.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: Review feedback was correct that file ownership alone was too vague. Naming the state model and fields makes shell cleanup implementable and testable.

resolved_open_questions.question: Does the public MVP wait for full family-sharing hook deletion?
resolved_open_questions.decision: No. The public MVP ships as the family-sharing transition release with fail-closed interception; full hook deletion is the cleanup release after the boundary gate passes.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: This is the safest non-destructive path while still removing sharing from the product surface immediately.

resolved_open_questions.question: What happens if Firebase analytics configuration is unavailable?
resolved_open_questions.decision: The app uses a no-op analytics adapter and continues shipping.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: Measurement is useful, but it cannot become a hidden blocker for a scope-reduction release.

resolved_open_questions.question: How is reminder retirement enforced consistently across future callers?
resolved_open_questions.decision: By a single persistence invariant that clears reminder fields on every production goal write and limits notification behavior to cancellation-only cleanup.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: Call-site cleanup alone is too fragile for a long-lived MVP codebase.

resolved_open_questions.question: How is family sharing neutralized in the public transition release without waiting for full deletion?
resolved_open_questions.decision: By disabling rollout by default, removing retained-surface refresh hooks and startup observation, reducing any surviving delegate hooks to fail-closed acceptance interception, and deleting the rest in the cleanup release after the boundary gate passes.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: UI removal alone would leave live runtime seams in production.

resolved_open_questions.question: How are out-of-shell Apple entry points handled in MVP?
resolved_open_questions.decision: Audit every production Scene/WindowGroup in CryptoSavingsTrackerApp.swift; keep only the main app window and macOS Settings window, and delete Goal Comparison before the public candidate.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: Route-absence claims must cover registered scene entry points, not just shared-shell navigation.

resolved_open_questions.question: What is the telemetry migration boundary for KPI measurement?
resolved_open_questions.decision: All KPI events flow through the app-owned analytics adapter, while AppLog-based trackers remain diagnostics-only unless explicitly wrapped by that adapter for retained journeys.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: This prevents parallel instrumentation stacks and conflicting KPI sources of truth.

resolved_open_questions.question: Is monetization part of the MVP decision scope?
resolved_open_questions.decision: No. Monetization is explicitly deferred to post-MVP roadmap planning.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: The current release objective is scope reduction, reliability, and validation of the retained core loop.

resolved_open_questions.question: Does the MVP architecture foreclose future monetization or entitlement gating?
resolved_open_questions.decision: No. The retained route boundaries, mutation services, and settings/help surfaces must stay compatible with future entitlement checks or paywall insertion.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: Monetization is out of scope for MVP delivery, but the pruning work should not hardcode an always-free structure that would force a later architectural reset.

resolved_open_questions.question: Where does final visual sign-off live for the MVP UI?
resolved_open_questions.decision: In this proposal artifact itself: the JSON design system, high-fidelity mockups, named error states, iconography rules, and motion tokens are the authoritative visual contract. External Figma comps translate and verify that contract but may not redefine it silently.
resolved_open_questions.reasoning: This closes the UI-review blocker without inventing a second source of truth.

open_questions: Generating the reusable .review-baselines/current-system-baseline.md artifact remains a pre-implementation documentation follow-up rather than part of this proposal-output pass.
The exact install-base and engagement denominator snapshot is still an execution dependency: Product Owner owns capture by 2026-04-26 before P2 closes and no later than the P4 gate.

platform_scope.release_scope_decision: The customer-facing MVP release targets iOS, macOS, and Android. visionOS remains compile-only shared-shell parity because /Users/user/Documents/CryptoSavingsTracker/CLAUDE.md states that platform capabilities exist but UI is not yet implemented there. Shared Apple code still must not preserve removed planning/sharing/export surfaces on visionOS builds.

platforms.shell: ContentView -> iOSContentView using NavigationStack, with DashboardView, goal detail, add/edit flows, and SettingsView as the primary retained surfaces.
ContentView -> macOSContentView using NavigationSplitView, plus shared Apple goal/dashboard/settings surfaces compiled from the same SwiftUI target.

out_of_shell_entry_points.reason: Goal comparison is retired scope and must not remain reachable outside the shared shell.
out_of_shell_entry_points.verification: Route-absence validation fails if the window group is still registered in the production scene graph.

out_of_shell_entry_points.reason: Settings remains a retained MVP surface, but its content must match the stripped MVP settings contract.
out_of_shell_entry_points.verification: Dedicated macOS Settings smoke must prove planning, sharing, bridge, export, and reminder controls are absent.

platforms.shell: Shared Apple SwiftUI shell compiled from the same retained dashboard/goal/settings code paths, but not treated as a customer-facing MVP release surface because repo guidance says UI is not yet implemented.
kept_routes: Compile-safe shared dashboard, goal detail, and settings views only insofar as they exist in shared Apple code.
platforms.release_gate: Compile validation and route-absence in shared code only; no end-user release readiness claim for visionOS in this MVP proposal.

platforms.shell: Bottom navigation with Dashboard, Goals, and Assets plus secondary routes for Settings, Goal detail, Goal dashboard, Add/Edit goal, Add/Edit asset, Transactions, and Allocations.

packaging_implications: No Apple-platform packaging split is introduced for MVP. The shared Apple target remains one product family, but only iOS and macOS are treated as customer-facing release-gated Apple surfaces in this proposal.
visionOS must continue compiling against the shared retained-surface contract and must not preserve removed planner-era or sharing routes, but it does not block MVP release on end-user UX readiness.
If an Apple-only debug or internal diagnostic surface remains, it must be guarded by build configuration and stay outside the production navigation graph on every Apple platform.
Android may land one sprint behind iOS, but it ships the same product contract rather than a narrower or broader variant.
Every production Scene/WindowGroup in CryptoSavingsTrackerApp.swift must be audited; the public MVP may keep only the main app window plus the dedicated Settings window on macOS.

out_of_shell_entry_points.reason: Goal comparison is removed product scope.
out_of_shell_entry_points.release_gate: Production build fails release validation if this registration survives.
Smoke validates that the kept window exposes only MVP-safe settings content.

localization_strategy.decision: English-only UI and release communications for MVP.
localization_strategy.reasoning: The immediate problem is reducing scope safely and rewriting copy away from planning/sharing/reminder language. One production language keeps QA, legal/store text, and migration messaging tractable for the MVP cut.
future_readiness: Retain copy catalogs and centralized strings as the only production copy sources so future localization does not require structural rewrites.
Design layouts and wireframes must tolerate text expansion even though only English ships at launch.
Russian or other locale additions are post-MVP follow-up work, not blocked by this proposal.

competitive_context: Generic crypto portfolio trackers such as CoinStats and Delta emphasize holdings visibility and market movement, not named savings-goal progress across wallet-linked and manual balances.

The MVP occupies the niche between market-first crypto trackers and general budgeting tools by combining explicit goal framing, optional on-chain address tracking, fiat/manual flexibility, and a progress-first dashboard instead of competing on breadth.

Before the first 30-day re-enablement review, the team captures one lightweight competitive and user-demand snapshot so any post-MVP breadth discussion is grounded in observed demand rather than pruning intuition alone.

support_article_outline.title: What changed in the CryptoSavingsTracker MVP update
support_article_outline.audience: Existing users updating from planning/sharing-capable builds.
support_article_outline.purpose: Give the migration banner, Settings help row, release notes, and store-review responses one consistent, empathetic explanation of retired features, preserved data, and the retained core workflow.

sections.title: What changed
required_points: The app now focuses on personal goal tracking, assets, balances, and dashboards.
Planning, reminders, family sharing, local bridge sync, and export tools are no longer part of the production MVP.

sections.title: What data is still here
required_points: Personal goals, assets, balances, and transactions remain preserved.
CloudKit data is not destructively migrated or deleted for this release.
Removed-feature records may still exist in storage but are no longer shown in normal production flows.
State plainly that existing data was not deleted even if some related UI is gone.

sections.title: How to use the streamlined app
required_points: Add a fiat or crypto asset.
Optionally add a wallet address for automatic tracking.
Record manual contributions and monitor progress from Dashboard or Goal Dashboard.

sections.title: Questions about removed features
required_points: Clarify that planning, reminder, and sharing surfaces were retired for reliability and focus.
Point users to support contact options if they are blocked by the streamlined scope.
Use empathetic copy that names the change first and then reassures users that preserved data is still safe.

acceptance_criteria: Readable in under three minutes on mobile.
Uses the same retirement language as the migration banner and release notes.
Explains preserved data without implying that retired UI paths still exist.
Available from the migration banner and from Settings/About for at least the first two post-MVP versions.
Uses empathetic language that reassures users about preserved data before explaining removed features.
Avoids implying that hidden retired-feature records are directly recoverable through normal MVP UI.
distribution: Settings > About > What changed in this update.
Release notes/store listing links where channel rules allow.
App-review/store-review response macros if clarification is required.
tone_requirements: Lead with reassurance about preserved goals, assets, balances, and transactions before listing removed surfaces.
Use plain language and avoid framing the change as user error or downgrade denial.
Acknowledge that existing users may have relied on planning, reminder, or sharing features and point them to support when that change is disruptive.

implementation_quick_reference.purpose: One-page companion for daily implementation and release coordination; the full proposal remains authoritative.
implementation_quick_reference.sequencing_rule: Finish Apple root-shell ownership, duplicate-host cleanup, and scene cleanup first, then reminder/family-sharing shutdown, then dashboard/root-dashboard v2 contract replacement, then dependency deletion and release hardening.

phase_cards.focus: Freeze scope, launch the parallel visual track, baseline-doc follow-up, support-article content, and rollout gates.
phase_cards.exit_gate: Inline visual/state coverage, cross-cutting retirement matrix, visual-translation role, support article outline, KPI rationale, baseline-doc owner, install-base snapshot owner, and quick-reference are approved.

phase_cards.focus: Apple root-shell cleanup, scene registration cleanup, navigation removal, and retirement coordinator insertion.
phase_cards.exit_gate: Dashboard-first shell is authoritative, duplicate MonthlyPlanningWidget hosts are cleaned, and no removed Apple entry point survives.

phase_cards.focus: Reminder/family-sharing shutdown plus dashboard v2 and crypto-tracking state implementation.
phase_cards.exit_gate: No live mutation path writes reminder behavior, retained dashboard/asset-tracking states match the inline mockups, and token-parity review for the translated visual comps passes.

phase_cards.focus: Dependency deletion, cleanup-release boundary work, and add-crypto-asset usability validation.
phase_cards.exit_gate: Transition-only family-sharing seams are ready for cleanup and usability findings are triaged.

phase_cards.focus: Release hardening, staged rollout, migration signal monitoring, and qualitative baseline capture.
phase_cards.exit_gate: Build/test gates pass, Wave 1 confusion signals stay below pause thresholds, and Android parity risk has either cleared or been explicitly escalated.

usage_guide.daily_start: Read the phase card for the current phase, then check technical_design.cross_cutting_retirement_matrix before touching shared notifications, sharing seams, reminder code, or widget hosts.
usage_guide.when_in_doubt: If code reality conflicts with the quick reference, the full proposal wins and should be updated explicitly if the contract must change.

post_mvp_direction.direction: If the MVP meets activation, retention, and reliability thresholds, the next product increment should prioritize depth over breadth for one cycle: improve the retained goal, dashboard, and crypto-tracking experience before selectively reconsidering retired features.
post_mvp_direction.why: The purpose of the MVP cut is to prove that a narrower personal-goal tracker earns repeat use. Shipping new breadth immediately would weaken that signal and recreate the same product sprawl the proposal is trying to remove.
post_mvp_direction.breadth_reconsideration_gate: Selective re-enablement is considered only after at least one full evidence window shows stable core-loop completion, retention, and meaningful retired-feature demand under the re-enablement framework.

how_to_read_this_proposal.daily_reference: implementation_quick_reference is the day-to-day companion; the full proposal remains authoritative for acceptance criteria, retirement contracts, and rollout gates.
change_highlights: Blocking review items are resolved in technical_design.cross_cutting_retirement_matrix, technical_design.apple_shell_navigation_contract, ux_ui_spec.visual_delivery_contract, and platform_scope.
Operational dependencies still pending execution are called out in rollout_plan.buffer_and_contingencies and open_questions rather than being hidden in prose.
